comments.body:
We actually want #1901.

But, let’s see how Go+Renovate interact… I’d like to try merging the release branch back into `main` to see if Go versioning can deal with that correctly.

So, please merge #1901 first, and then we’ll see how the tools react.
Filed https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1842 as a proposal for what to do, please see details there.

Nope, just merging the release branch into `main`, while vendoring the dependency before a merge, is not sufficient; Go, and Renovate, don’t automatically figure out that the right update point is the merge commit.

Let’s try that manually…
